tasks.spec:
You are a zero-cost mock mechanics worker proving Ringer's spawn/check/log loop before any real model spends tokens. Boundary: write only inside your current task directory; never touch anything else. Goal: produce alpha.txt containing exactly one line: 'ringer mechanics alpha ready'. This spec drives engines/mock_worker.py, which writes the MOCK_FILE block below verbatim into the task directory.
MOCK_FILE: alpha.txt
ringer mechanics alpha ready
MOCK_END
tasks.check: printf 'ringer mechanics alpha ready\n' | diff - alpha.txt && echo 'alpha.txt matches expected content exactly'
tasks.verified: alpha.txt exists and byte-for-byte matches the expected single line (diff, not diff -q, so a mismatch prints the difference)

tasks.spec:
You are a zero-cost mock mechanics worker proving Ringer handles nested-directory deliverables. Boundary: write only inside your current task directory. Goal: produce report/beta.md — a two-section markdown report proving directory creation works. This spec drives engines/mock_worker.py, which writes the MOCK_FILE block below verbatim (creating parent directories as needed).
MOCK_FILE: report/beta.md
# Beta mechanics report

## Findings
Nested directory write path works: this file was created at report/beta.md by the mock worker.

## Verdict
mechanics-pass
MOCK_END
tasks.check: grep -qi '^## findings' report/beta.md || { echo 'FAIL: missing Findings section in report/beta.md'; exit 1; }; grep -qi '^## verdict' report/beta.md || { echo 'FAIL: missing Verdict section'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'mechanics-pass' report/beta.md || { echo 'FAIL: verdict marker mechanics-pass absent'; exit 1; }; echo 'beta.md has Findings + Verdict sections and the mechanics-pass marker'
tasks.verified: report/beta.md exists in a nested directory and contains Findings and Verdict sections plus the mechanics-pass marker (each missing piece named on failure)

tasks.spec:
You are a zero-cost mock mechanics worker proving Ringer's checks can execute an artifact, not just see it exists. Boundary: write only inside your current task directory. Goal: produce gamma.json — a machine-parseable status file. This spec drives engines/mock_worker.py, which writes the MOCK_FILE block below verbatim.
MOCK_FILE: gamma.json
{"component": "ringer-mechanics", "status": "ready", "phase": 2}
MOCK_END
tasks.check: python3 -m json.tool gamma.json > /dev/null || { echo 'FAIL: gamma.json is not valid JSON:'; cat gamma.json; exit 1; }; python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('gamma.json')); sys.exit(0 if d.get('status')=='ready' else ('FAIL: status is %r, expected ready' % d.get('status'), 1)[1])" && echo 'gamma.json parses and status==ready'
tasks.verified: gamma.json parses as real JSON and its status field equals 'ready' — the check executes the artifact through a parser rather than trusting existence
